rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Jennifer Kwoks Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondent's unopposed request for judicial notice is GRANTED. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The Court conducted an in camera review of four documents, all titled "Memo to File" and dated: 1) September 14, 2009; 2) October 23, 2009; 3) November 23, 2009; and 4) March 16, 2010. In the copy provided to the Court, the second paragraph of the document dated September 14, 2009, was partially redacted. Petitioner's Motion to Compel Production of Documents is DENIED as to the documents dated October 28, 2009, and March 16, 2010. The holder of the attorney work product privilege in California is the attorney. A client cannot waive the attorney work product privilege in California; therefore, Respondent's assertion of an advice of counsel defense does not waive the attorney work product privilege. See Lasky, Haas, Cohlet & Munter v. Sup. Ct. (Getty) (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 264, 279; Lohman v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 81 Cal.App.3d 90, 101. The Court has determined that these documents are attorney work product. Since the privilege has not been waived, these documents are protected from discovery. Petitioner's Motion to Compel Production of Documents is GRANTED as to the documents dated September 14, 2009, and November 23, 2009, submitted to the Court for in camera review. These documents are nonderivative materials that simply memorialize communications the attorney had with his client. As such, they are not attorney work product despite any attorney "work" that went into typing them up. See Mach v. Sup. Ct. (State of Calif.) (1968) 259 Cal.App.2d 7, 10; Aerojet-General Corp. v. Transport Indem. Ins. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 996, 1004; Huffy Corp. v. St.p. Ct. (Winterhur Swiss Ins. Co.) (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 97, 109. While these particular documents may generally be subject to the attorney-client privilege, the attorney-client privilege has been waived by Respondent's advice of counsel affirmative defense. Accordingly, Respondent is ordered to produce the September 14, 2009, and the November 23, 2009, documents to Petitioner, within five days of this order. Both sanctions requests are denied. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Judgment Based Upon Stipulated Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. The Hearing on Movant Richard Keith Suarez's Motion for Entry of Judgment Based on Stipulated Settlement Agreement is denied in part and granted in part. The motion is DENIED as to reimbursement for amounts purported paid to Madison Painting, ROB Painting, and Manbel Scaffold. Richard Keith Suarez has not shown that the terms of the Settlement Agreement have been satisfied; the support documentation provided by Richard Keith Suarez was insufficient to verify: 1) the businesses to which payments were made; 2) for what services payments were made; 3) the exact amounts of payments; and 4) from whom the payments originated. The motion is GRANTED as to the $220 payment to Barbara's Locksmith. Stacei Suarez is ordered to pay $220 to Richard Keith Suarez as reimbursement for Richard Keith Suarez's expenditure of the funds for the improvement of the real property at 3252-3254 16th Street, San Francisco. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
